ucs.title: [LLM] Cl0p LEMURLOOT (human2.aspx) web shell drop on MOVEit / GoAnywhere / Cleo MFT hosts
ucs.description: Hunts the IIS worker process (w3wp.exe) writing an .aspx/.ashx file into the application root of a managed-file-transfer product (MOVEit Transfer, GoAnywhere MFT, Cleo Harmony/VLTrader/LexiCom). This is the exact artifact Cl0p / FIN11 / GRACEFUL SPIDER drops after exploiting CVE-2023-34362 (MOVEit), CVE-2023-0669 (GoAnywhere) and CVE-2024-50623 (Cleo) — the LEMURLOOT/'human2.aspx' family is the actor's signature initial-access webshell.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.process_name="w3wp.exe" AND (Filesystem.file_name="*.aspx" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.ashx") AND (Filesystem.file_path="*\\MOVEitTransfer\\wwwroot\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\GoAnywhere\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\Cleo\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\Harmony\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\VLTrader\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\LexiCom\\*") by host Filesystem.file_path Filesystem.file_name Filesystem.user Filesystem.process_guid | `drop_dm_object_name("Filesystem")` | eval lemurloot_signature=if(match(file_name,"(?i)^(_?human2?|app_web_[a-z0-9]{8}|LEMURLOOT)\.(aspx|ashx)$"),1,0) | where lemurloot_signature=1 OR file_path LIKE "%\\MOVEitTransfer\\wwwroot\\%" | convert ctime(firstTime), ctime(lastTime) | sort -lastTime
ucs.kql:
// Cl0p LEMURLOOT — IIS worker writing .aspx into an MFT application root
let _mft_paths = dynamic([@"MOVEitTransfer\wwwroot", @"GoAnywhere", @"\Cleo\", @"\Harmony\", @"\VLTrader\", @"\LexiCom\"]);
let _lemurloot_names = dynamic(["human.aspx","human2.aspx","_human2.aspx","_human.aspx"]);
DeviceFileEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(30d)
| where ActionType in ("FileCreated","FileModified","FileRenamed")
| where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "w3wp.exe"             // IIS worker
| where FileName endswith ".aspx" or FileName endswith ".ashx" or FileName endswith ".dll"
| where FolderPath has_any (_mft_paths)                     // MFT app root
| extend IsKnownLemurloot = FileName in~ (_lemurloot_names)
      or FileName matches regex @"(?i)^(_?human2?|app_web_[a-f0-9]{8})\.(aspx|ashx)$"
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, FolderPath, FileName, SHA256, MD5,
          InitiatingProcessAccountName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          IsKnownLemurloot
| order by IsKnownLemurloot desc, Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Cl0p LEMURLOOT — IIS worker writing .aspx into an MFT application root
let _mft_paths = dynamic([@"MOVEitTransfer\wwwroot", @"GoAnywhere", @"\Cleo\", @"\Harmony\", @"\VLTrader\", @"\LexiCom\"]);
let _lemurloot_names = dynamic(["human.aspx","human2.aspx","_human2.aspx","_human.aspx"]);
ImFileEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where EventType in ("FileCreated","FileModified","FileRenamed")
| where ActingProcessName has "w3wp.exe"
| where TargetFileName endswith ".aspx" or TargetFileName endswith ".ashx" or TargetFileName endswith ".dll"
| where TargetFilePath has_any (_mft_paths)
| extend IsKnownLemurloot = TargetFileName in~ (_lemurloot_names)
      or TargetFileName matches regex @"(?i)^(_?human2?|app_web_[a-f0-9]{8})\.(aspx|ashx)$"
| project TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, TargetFilePath, TargetFileName,
          TargetFileSHA256, ActorUsername, ActingProcessCommandLine,
          IsKnownLemurloot
| order by IsKnownLemurloot desc, TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Cl0p LEMURLOOT Web Shell Written to MFT Application Root
id: 7e9c2d11-cl0p-lemurloot-mft
status: experimental
description: Detects IIS worker (w3wp.exe) writing an .aspx/.ashx into MOVEit Transfer, GoAnywhere MFT, or Cleo Harmony/VLTrader/LexiCom application directories — the signature LEMURLOOT/human2.aspx web shell drop used by Cl0p (FIN11 / GRACEFUL SPIDER) during MOVEit (CVE-2023-34362), GoAnywhere (CVE-2023-0669) and Cleo (CVE-2024-50623) mass-exploitation campaigns.
references:
  - https://www.cisa.gov/news-events/cybersecurity-advisories/aa23-158a
  - https://www.mandiant.com/resources/blog/lemurloot-moveit-vulnerability-clop
author: Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/07
logsource:
  product: windows
  category: file_event
detection:
  selection_writer:
    Image|endswith: '\w3wp.exe'
    TargetFilename|endswith:
      - '.aspx'
      - '.ashx'
      - '.dll'
  selection_path:
    TargetFilename|contains:
      - '\MOVEitTransfer\wwwroot\'
      - '\GoAnywhere\'
      - '\Cleo\'
      - '\Harmony\'
      - '\VLTrader\'
      - '\LexiCom\'
  condition: selection_writer and selection_path
falsepositives:
  - Vendor-signed MFT product upgrades (rare; verify SHA256 against vendor catalog)
level: high
tags:
  - attack.initial_access
  - attack.t1190
  - attack.persistence
  - attack.t1505.003
  - threat.actor.cl0p
  - threat.actor.fin11
ucs.rationale: LEMURLOOT/human2.aspx is THE canonical Cl0p artifact: CISA AA23-158A and Mandiant's LEMURLOOT report both pin this exact file/process pair (w3wp.exe writing human2.aspx into MOVEitTransfer\wwwroot) to FIN11/Cl0p. The actor uses the same w3wp-drops-aspx pattern across all three of their mass-exploitation MFT campaigns. The narrow context (IIS worker + MFT product path + aspx extension) yields almos
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID

ucs.title: [LLM] Cl0p post-exploitation — IIS worker on MFT host spawning interpreters & recon LOLBins
ucs.description: Hunts the second stage of Cl0p's MFT-exploit chain: after the LEMURLOOT/human2.aspx shell is dropped, the IIS worker on the MFT host spawns cmd/powershell/mshta/certutil/rundll32 plus discovery binaries (whoami, nltest, net, systeminfo) under the SYSTEM-equivalent IIS app-pool identity. This parent→child shape on a MOVEit/GoAnywhere/Cleo box is Cl0p's hands-on-keyboard signal before TrueBot/Cobalt-Strike staging and bulk data theft.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdlines from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name="w3wp.exe" AND (Processes.parent_process="*MOVEitDMZ*" OR Processes.parent_process="*MOVEitTransfer*" OR Processes.parent_process="*GoAnywhere*" OR Processes.parent_process="*\\Cleo\\*" OR Processes.parent_process="*VLTrader*" OR Processes.parent_process="*LexiCom*" OR Processes.parent_process="*Harmony*") AND Processes.process_name IN ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","bitsadmin.exe","certutil.exe","curl.exe","msiexec.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","whoami.exe","nltest.exe","systeminfo.exe","tasklist.exe","schtasks.exe","hostname.exe") by host Processes.user Processes.parent_process Processes.process_name Processes.process | `drop_dm_object_name("Processes")` | sort -lastTime
ucs.kql:
// Cl0p — w3wp.exe on an MFT host spawning interpreters / recon LOLBins
let _mft_markers = dynamic(["MOVEitDMZ","MOVEitTransfer","GoAnywhere","\\Cleo\\","VLTrader","LexiCom","Harmony"]);
let _interp_lolbins = dynamic([
    "cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe",
    "regsvr32.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","bitsadmin.exe",
    "certutil.exe","curl.exe","wget.exe","msiexec.exe",
    "net.exe","net1.exe","whoami.exe","nltest.exe",
    "systeminfo.exe","tasklist.exe","schtasks.exe","hostname.exe","reg.exe"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "w3wp.exe"
| where InitiatingProcessCommandLine has_any (_mft_markers)
   or InitiatingProcessFolderPath has_any (_mft_markers)
| where FileName in~ (_interp_lolbins)
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          ParentImage = InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
          ParentCmd   = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          ChildImage  = FolderPath,
          ChildName   = FileName,
          ChildCmd    = ProcessCommandLine,
          SHA256, IsRemoteSession = InitiatingProcessTokenElevation
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Cl0p — IIS worker on MFT host spawning interpreters / recon LOLBins
let _mft_markers = dynamic(["MOVEitDMZ","MOVEitTransfer","GoAnywhere","\\Cleo\\","VLTrader","LexiCom","Harmony"]);
let _interp_lolbins = dynamic([
    "cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe",
    "regsvr32.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","bitsadmin.exe",
    "certutil.exe","curl.exe","wget.exe","msiexec.exe",
    "net.exe","net1.exe","whoami.exe","nltest.exe",
    "systeminfo.exe","tasklist.exe","schtasks.exe","hostname.exe","reg.exe"]);
ImProcessCreate
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where ParentProcessName has "w3wp.exe"
| where ParentProcessCommandLine has_any (_mft_markers)
| extend ChildBin = tolower(tostring(split(TargetProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
| where ChildBin in~ (_interp_lolbins)
| project TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, ActorUsername,
          ParentProcessCommandLine,
          TargetProcessName, TargetProcessCommandLine,
          Hash
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Cl0p Hands-on-Keyboard via MFT IIS Worker (w3wp.exe Spawning Interpreters)
id: 9b41ee30-cl0p-mft-w3wp-children
status: experimental
description: Detects an IIS worker process (w3wp.exe) on a MOVEit / GoAnywhere / Cleo MFT host spawning interpreters or discovery LOLBins — the post-LEMURLOOT hands-on-keyboard stage of a Cl0p (FIN11 / TA505) intrusion.
references:
  - https://www.cisa.gov/news-events/cybersecurity-advisories/aa23-158a
  - https://www.huntress.com/blog/cleo-software-actively-being-exploited-in-the-wild
author: Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/07
logsource:
  product: windows
  category: process_creation
detection:
  parent_iis:
    ParentImage|endswith: '\w3wp.exe'
  parent_mft:
    ParentCommandLine|contains:
      - 'MOVEitDMZ'
      - 'MOVEitTransfer'
      - 'GoAnywhere'
      - '\Cleo\'
      - 'VLTrader'
      - 'LexiCom'
      - 'Harmony'
  child_lolbins:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
      - '\mshta.exe'
      - '\rundll32.exe'
      - '\regsvr32.exe'
      - '\wscript.exe'
      - '\cscript.exe'
      - '\bitsadmin.exe'
      - '\certutil.exe'
      - '\curl.exe'
      - '\msiexec.exe'
      - '\net.exe'
      - '\net1.exe'
      - '\whoami.exe'
      - '\nltest.exe'
      - '\systeminfo.exe'
      - '\tasklist.exe'
      - '\schtasks.exe'
      - '\reg.exe'
  condition: parent_iis and parent_mft and child_lolbins
falsepositives:
  - MFT product installer / patch scripts (rare; verify cmdline matches vendor patcher)
level: high
tags:
  - attack.initial_access
  - attack.t1190
  - attack.execution
  - attack.t1059.001
  - attack.t1059.003
  - attack.discovery
  - attack.t1087
  - attack.t1069
  - threat.actor.cl0p
  - threat.actor.fin11
  - threat.actor.ta505
ucs.rationale: Mandiant, Microsoft (Lace Tempest / Spandex Tempest writeups) and CISA all describe the same Cl0p sequence on MOVEit/GoAnywhere/Cleo: web-shell drop → web-shell triggers w3wp.exe to launch cmd/powershell with embedded `whoami`, `net group "Domain Admins"`, `nltest /domain_trusts`, certutil downloads, then TrueBot or Cobalt-Strike staging via msiexec/rundll32. IIS workers on dedicated MFT appliance
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID
